mnemonics.title: Delivery Semantics Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Ats: Most (0-1), Least (1+), Exactly (1) - Think of numbers: 0, 1+, 1 exactly. M-A-O = May be Zero, L-O-M = Lots of Messages, E-O = Exactly One

acronyms.usage: Protocol enabling execution of procedures on remote machines
Language-independent way to define function signatures for RPC
Architectural style using request-reply over HTTP
Idempotent HTTP method - putting same resource twice is safe

visualTips.visualization: Draw arrow from Client → Server (labeled 'Request'), wait arrow (labeled 'Processing'), arrow from Server → Client (labeled 'Response'). Shows blocking nature in sync mode.
Three boxes: At-most-once (circle with hole = lost ok), At-least-once (circle full + duplicates = many circles), Exactly-once (single circle = perfect)

Two buildings (Client, Server). Client has stub (small box), Server has skeleton (small box). Network cloud in between. Arrows show: Call → Marshal → Send → Unmarshal → Execute → Marshal → Send → Unmarshal → Return.

analogies.analogy: Phone call - you dial, wait, speak, wait for response, then hang up. Cannot do another call simultaneously.
Email - send message, continue working, check for reply later. Or leaving voicemail and calling back.
Like setting a light switch - turning it ON multiple times has same effect as once. But adding sugar to coffee twice makes it sweeter (not idempotent).
